errors: The "ProcedureLookBack" function uses a LookBack interval of 1 month for colonoscopy screening, which is not clinically reasonable. Colonoscopies for average-risk patients are recommended every 10 years, not monthly. This would lead to over-screening and incorrect eligibility determination. The LookBack parameter should be set to 10 years (e.g., 10 "year" units) to align with standard guidelines.

["The 'ProcedureLookBack' function uses a 1-month look-back interval for prostate cancer screening procedures. This is clinically unreasonable as standard guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) recommend screening intervals of 1-2 years for men aged 55-69 after shared decision-making. A 1-month interval would likely trigger premature or redundant recommendations."]

The HbA1c threshold for diabetes diagnosis is set to 9% (g%), which is clinically unreasonable. The standard diagnostic threshold for HbA1c is ≥6.5% according to major guidelines (e.g., ADA, AACE). Using 9% would significantly delay appropriate diagnosis and intervention.

["Has Smoking History" expression does not verify 20 pack-year smoking history as required by USPSTF guidelines. The current implementation only checks for active tobacco use status without quantifying duration/intensity.]

[Minimum Age criteria uses 60-75 years instead of standard 45-75 years for average-risk colorectal cancer screening. Has Recent Colorectal Screening checks for screening within 2 years, but standard guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) recommend intervals of 8-10 years for colonoscopy or every 1-2 years for stool-based tests, depending on modality. The 2-year window may not align with evidence-based intervals.]

["In Demographic" condition uses an age threshold of 30 years, which may not align with clinical guidelines recommending broader age ranges (e.g., CDC recommends HIV screening for all adults 13-64 years regardless of age). The 30-year cutoff could exclude younger adults who may be at risk and should be justified by specific local or guideline-based criteria.]

["In Demographic expression checks age >=30 instead of USPSTF-recommended 21. Rationale mentions 21-65 but logic uses 30-65. This excludes women aged 21-29 who should be screened according to USPSTF guidelines."]

["In Demographic" expression uses age ≥75 years, but USPSTF recommends falls prevention screening for adults ≥65 years at increased risk. The age threshold in the ELM (75) is higher than the clinical guideline (65), excluding eligible patients aged 65-74 from the recommendation."]

["In Demographic" expression uses an age threshold of 25 years, but the rationale cites USPSTF recommendations which specify screening for adults 18 years or older. This creates a clinical inconsistency between the eligibility criteria and the stated guideline reference.]

["In Demographic" checks age ≥55 and ≤75, but USPSTF recommends AAA screening for men aged 65-75 who have smoked. The lower age threshold should be 65, not 55. The "Rationale" correctly references 65-75, creating a discrepancy with the actual demographic criteria.]

["Needs Tobacco Use Screening" expression incorrectly references only "In Demographic" without incorporating "Has Recent Tobacco Screening" logic. The clinical recommendation should require both age eligibility (>=18) AND absence of a recent screening (within past year) to trigger the recommendation, but the current ELM lacks the latter check, leading to potential over-recommendation for patients already screened."]

["Needs Falls Prevention Screening" incorrectly combines "In Demographic" (age ≥65) with "Has Recent Falls Assessment" (exists in past year). This logic would recommend screening only for patients who already have a recent assessment, which is contradictory.
